name: Prof. Sarah Chen
title: Professor of Psychiatry
bio:
Prof. Sarah Chen is a leading psychiatrist and neuroscientist at UNSW Sydney and the Randwick Health & Innovation Precinct. Her work focuses on treatment-resistant depression and neurostimulation therapies, including transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) and ketamine-based approaches.

She leads the Precinct's Treatment-Resistant Depression Clinic at Prince of Wales Hospital and has established one of Australia's largest clinical trial programmes in neurostimulation. Her research bridges mechanistic neuroscience with real-world clinical outcomes.

Prof. Chen has published over 140 peer-reviewed articles and holds several patents related to neurostimulation device protocols and patient monitoring systems.

patents.title: Adaptive TMS dosing protocol for treatment-resistant depression
Patient monitoring system for ketamine infusion therapy
Combined neurostimulation and pharmacotherapy treatment method

news.title: UNSW team launches Australia's largest TMS depression trial
news.summary: Prof. Chen leads a multi-site trial evaluating personalised TMS protocols for patients who have not responded to standard antidepressants.

news.title: Ketamine therapy guidelines published for Australian clinicians
news.summary: New clinical guidelines co-authored by Prof. Chen provide evidence-based recommendations for ketamine-assisted treatment in mood disorders.

news.title: RHIP Precinct opens dedicated neurostimulation research unit
news.summary: The new unit, led by Prof. Chen, integrates clinical care with translational research on brain stimulation therapies.

awards.title: Royal Australian and New Zealand College of Psychiatrists Research Prize
